context:
I'll gather all the necessary market data simultaneously. Let me run multiple searches right now.
Here is your morning market briefing for Thursday, June 18, 2026:

---

**US MARKET BRIEFING — Thursday, June 18, 2026**

---

**Yesterday recap:**
- 
S&P 500: 7,420.10 (-1.21%)

- 
Nasdaq: 26,021.66 (-1.34%)

- 
Russell 2000: 2,917.98 (-0.72%)

- Key events: 
Declines were owed to commentary after the FOMC meeting, which revealed an increasing bias among Fed members to raise rates later this year. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh did little to settle those projections, even though he said none of the Fed participants felt the need to raise rates today.
 
Policymakers' dot plot revealed that nine out of 18 Fed officials now see interest rates increasing in 2026. Complicating the forecast was Warsh's decision to abstain from submitting a rate forecast.
 
The 10Y Treasury yield jumped 6.9 bps to 4.497%.

- Notable after-hours: 
S&P 500 futures +0.63%, Nasdaq 100 futures +1.03%, Russell 2000 futures +0.88% in after-hours trade.
 
INTC (Intel) +3.46% in pre-market; ACN (Accenture) -9.51% after reporting earnings.


---

**Pre-market / overnight:**
- Futures: 
Stock futures were higher and oil prices were sliding Thursday as investors reacted to the Federal Reserve's latest interest-rate decision and developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict.
 ES ~+0.6%, NQ ~+1.0%, RTY ~+0.9% (per after-hours index moves).
- VIX: 
VIX closed at 18.44, up +12.37% on the session.
 Pre-market VIX is pulling back; 
S&P 500 VIX indicated near 16.11 in early Thursday trade, -0.56%.

- Rates/FX/Commodities: 
As of market close June 17, the yield curve is upward-sloping. The 2-Year Treasury yield is 4.20%, and the 10-Year Treasury yield is 4.49%.
 
DXY near 100.09–100.81 range (near 2-month high).
 
WTI crude fell to $74.56/bbl on June 18, down ~2.91% from the prior day.
 
Gold held below $4,300/oz on Thursday, recovering losses from the prior session after Trump signed an interim agreement to end the conflict with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

- Asia/Europe overnight: 
Japan's Nikkei 225 rose 1.65% to close at a record 71,053.49.
 
South Korea's KOSPI jumped to a fresh record, rising 2.3%; Hong Kong's Hang Seng fell 1.6%, while mainland China's CSI 300 added 0.21%.
 
European markets opened in mixed territory; the FTSE 100 was 0.65% lower, the French CAC 40 was up 0.03%, and Germany's DAX rose 0.27%.
 
Interest rate decisions from the Bank of England and the Swiss National Bank are due later.


---

**Today's setup:**
- Regime: **Bounce / risk-on recovery** — markets attempting to digest the hawkish Fed shock and pivot toward the Iran deal tailwind.
- Expected gap direction at open: **Gap up** — 
futures linked to the main indices on Wall Street edged up after stocks sank in the wake of the first Federal Reserve rate decision under Warsh; oil prices fell 2% on Thursday to their lowest since the start of the conflict.

- Active themes likely to move: AI/semis recovery bid, energy/oil supply normalization, rate-sensitive small caps (RTY outperforming), dollar strength headwind for multinationals.
- Risks / unbroken patterns: 
The S&P 500 shed 1.21% on Wednesday — the worst performance for the index on the first "Fed day" under a new chair since 1994.
 Yields remain elevated at 4.49% on the 10Y. 
The 2-year yield rocketed higher as many Fed officials signaled a possible hike this year.
 Watch for any further Warsh commentary or Fed speaker headlines. Note: 
US markets are closed Thursday June 19 for the Juneteenth holiday
 — today is the last full trading day of the week.

---

**Active themes to watch:**
- **Fed hawkishness / rate hike risk:** 
Half of the FOMC projected it may be necessary to raise interest rates this year, consistent with expectations that core inflation will be higher than expected due to the impact of the war in the Middle East.
 Rate-sensitive sectors (real estate, utilities, small caps) remain in focus. Watch TLT, IWM, XRE.
- **US-Iran deal / oil normalization:** 
President Trump signed the U.S.-Iran agreement at the Palace of Versailles in France. "It's signed," Trump told reporters. The document was also signed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian.
 
Crude prices have fallen nearly 40% from their conflict peak as geopolitical risk fades, easing inflation concerns and helping consumers.
 Watch XLE, refiners, airlines (UAL, DAL), and consumer discretionary.
- **AI / Semis rebound:** 
Weakness in Magnificent Seven technology stocks and a slump in software stocks weighed on the overall market Wednesday; stocks initially moved higher on strength in chipmakers.
 
SK Hynix advanced 3.45% to a fresh high in Seoul overnight.
 Watch NVDA, AMD, INTC (pre-market +3.46%), AMAT, MRVL.

---

**Yesterday's notable movers (top 5):**
- 
All 11 GICS sectors ended lower Wednesday. Communication services led losses at -2.98%, followed by consumer discretionary (-2.69%) and real estate (-2.47%).

- INTC (Intel) — 
+3.46% pre-market Thursday
, recovering after chip sector weakness.
- ACN (Accenture) — 
-9.51% after reporting earnings
, a significant miss/guidance cut.
- 
VIX surged 12% on the day
, reflecting the sharp risk-off repricing post-FOMC.
- 
Gold tumbled nearly 2% on Wednesday after the Fed signaled increasing support for interest rate hikes this year.


---

**Today's earnings / scheduled catalysts:**
- Pre-market: 
Accenture (ACN) and Kroger (KR) are reporting today.
 ACN already gapping down sharply (-9.51%) — watch for read-through to IT services peers (CTSH, INFY). KR — watch for consumer spending signals amid inflation backdrop.
- After-close: No major S&P 500 names expected tonight per available calendars. 
US markets are closed Friday June 19 for Juneteenth
 — position sizing and hedging into the long weekend will be a factor all session.
- Macro: 
Bank of England and Swiss National Bank rate decisions are due today
 — potential FX volatility in GBP and CHF pairs with knock-on effects for US multinationals.

---

*Note: Pre-market futures levels are approximate based on after-hours index moves. Confirm live quotes at open. All data sourced from real-time and near-real-time feeds as of early June 18, 2026 ET.*

candidates.name: NVIDIA Corporation
candidates.reasoning: Mag 7 software/comm weighed on yesterday's selloff but chipmakers initially led the bounce; with futures sharply higher and SK Hynix hitting fresh record overnight on HBM4E samples, NVDA is the AI-infra bellwether positioned for risk-on recovery. Has lagged peers YTD, leaving room to run on a relief rally.

candidates.name: Broadcom Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Broadcom gained 4.3% in Wednesday pre-market on the chip rebound and remains Morgan Stanley's top semi pick on custom-ASIC AI demand; SK Hynix HBM4E sample milestone overnight supports the AI infrastructure narrative for today's risk-on session.

candidates.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Direct beneficiary of SK Hynix's HBM4E sample delivery milestone overnight as HBM memory peer; Micron has been outperforming airlines in pre-market on rotation into economically sensitive names with AI exposure on risk-on tape.

candidates.name: Apple Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Trump posted on Truth Social Thursday that Apple has agreed to work with Intel on U.S. chip production — a fresh, regular-session-eligible catalyst that aligns with onshoring/reshoring policy tailwind and supports relative strength vs SPY into the open.

candidates.name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Communication services sector led losses yesterday at -2.98% on the hawkish FOMC reaction; META as the largest comm-services constituent sets up for an oversold bounce as NQ futures lead the rebound (+1.0%) and VIX cools sub-17.

candidates.name: Delta Air Lines, Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Delta broke out to a new high on Monday and is the least fuel-exposed major with the strongest balance sheet, making it the cleanest individual airline beneficiary as WTI falls another ~2.5% Thursday to ~$74.50 on the formally signed Iran agreement.

candidates.name: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd.
candidates.reasoning: Cruise lines are highly fuel-exposed; with the Iran deal now formally signed at Versailles and WTI making fresh post-conflict lows Thursday, RCL — which led cruises on Monday with +5% — gets a second catalyst as the Strait reopening becomes contractual rather than rumored.

candidates.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
candidates.reasoning: Hawkish dot plot with 9/18 Fed officials seeing 2026 hikes pushed the 2Y to 4.20% and 10Y to 4.497% — a steeper curve and 'higher-for-longer' regime is structurally supportive of big-bank net interest margins. JPM as the highest-quality money-center bank gets the rotation flow.

candidates.name: Coinbase Global, Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Crypto-exposed equities have been gaining alongside Bitcoin's risk-on rebound during this Iran-deal-driven rally; with NQ futures +1.0% and VIX collapsing, COIN typically prints high-beta moves vs QQQ on bounce days.

candidates.name: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd.
candidates.reasoning: Asia tape was firmly risk-on overnight with Nikkei at a record 71,053 (+1.65%) and KOSPI breaking 9,000 for the first time ever (+2.3%); BABA as the largest-liquidity China ADR catches the regional risk-on flow and benefits from a weaker risk-off bid (cooling VIX, falling oil).

scored.reasoning:
NVDA was named in Trump's Truth Social post as already having agreed to build first-level chips with Intel, giving it a fresh policy-tailwind mention alongside the AI infrastructure rebound bid. 
Trump said 'First, we helped bring in Nvidia, and they agreed to build their first level Chips with Intel'
, and the stock has underperformed semis recently, leaving room for mean reversion on a risk-on tape.

JPMorgan said investors should be 'aggressive buyers' of Broadcom stock
, providing a fresh analyst-driven catalyst into a chip sector rebound day. Theme aligned with the semi rally but not the leader (Intel/NVDA are getting the policy spotlight).

Memory pricing narrative is gaining traction with 
Apple's planned price hikes showing memory chips have more room to run, per Gene Munster
, and Tim Cook's WSJ comments about unsustainable memory cost increases align directly with Micron's pricing leverage. Theme aligned but not the chip-rally leader today.

Major tech bellwethers led Wednesday's losses, with Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Alphabet and Amazon all closing in the red
, setting up META for an oversold bounce as NQ futures lead the rebound and VIX cools. Single-signal setup without a stock-specific catalyst.

Banks tend to do better in a moderately higher interest rate environment per Jim Cramer, and financials have the market's deepest bench of near-breakouts
. Higher-for-longer regime supports NIM, but 
JPMorgan rose more than 1% to a new all-time intraday high on Wednesday — the first new ATH since January 5
, meaning the stock is already extended into the move.
Crypto-beta name positioned for high-beta participation in the risk-on bounce as NQ futures lead and VIX collapses post-Fed shock. Single-theme alignment without a stock-specific catalyst today.

Futures suggest investors are looking past the Fed shock, with stocks in Asia mostly higher overnight and Japan's Nikkei 225 and South Korea's Kospi both hitting fresh records
, supporting risk-on flow into liquid China ADRs. Theme alignment only without a BABA-specific catalyst.

Trump's Truth Social post hit in the overnight hours (~12:44 AM ET / 3:22 AM PT) before the pre-market open, making this a stale pre-open catalyst pattern — 
Apple shares only gained 0.6% in reaction, with INTC capturing nearly all the move
. Catalyst is essentially priced in for AAPL while the read-through value flows to Intel.

, and the stock has underperformed semis recently, leaving room for mean reversion on a risk-on tape.

candidates.name: Rambus Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Memory-interface IP designer benefits from the broad semi rally; the Roundhill Memory ETF jumped 6.2% in premarket on Apple-Intel halo, and Rambus is squarely in the memory-interface segment.

candidates.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Semi metrology/process-control mid-cap leveraged to Intel 18A-P ramp and broader US fab buildout; SOXX up 4.6% premarket on Apple-Intel news lifts the entire equipment cohort.

candidates.name: FormFactor Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Mid-cap HBM/wafer test play; Zacks #1 with strong HBM4 ramp narrative, gets sympathy bid on the chip sector rally without the crowding of mega-caps.

candidates.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
candidates.reasoning: Defense/space mid-cap not directly tied to Iran-peace fade trade; benefits from sustained Pentagon space and unmanned systems spending; cleaner setup than already-extended RKLB.

candidates.name: AeroVironment Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Drone/unmanned-systems mid-cap; defense backlog story remains intact even with Iran deal as DoD modernization is multi-year; offers diversification from the chip cluster.

candidates.name: Upstart Holdings Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Rate-sensitive consumer lender; Russell 2000 outperformance signal and 10Y pullback from yesterday's 4.497% peak helps consumer-lending mid-cap names. Up 4% in recent session showing relative strength.

candidates.name: Exelixis Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Profitable oncology mid-cap with cabozantinib franchise; defensive biotech that benefits when rate-sensitive growth groups rotate. Diversifies portfolio away from chip cluster.

candidates.name: Viking Therapeutics Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Obesity/GLP-1 mid-cap biotech with high day-trader range; risk-on bounce regime favors high-beta biotech with no fresh overnight catalyst to fade.

candidates.name: Cipher Mining Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Bitcoin miner pivoting to AI/HPC hosting; VanEck identified it as one of the AI-pivot beneficiaries with hyperscaler-grade tenant quality. Mid-cap exposure to dual AI-infra + crypto narrative without IREN's stretched valuation.

candidates.name: Argan Inc.
candidates.reasoning: Power infrastructure EPC mid-cap; pure play on AI data-center power buildout that S&P Global called ~80% of private domestic demand growth in 1H 2025; uncorrelated to the chip/airline rotations.

. 
RMBS jumped 13.5% on June 2 on AI-memory roadmap optimism
, showing the name is already a recognized theme beneficiary, though no fresh stock-specific catalyst today.

Rate-sensitive AI lender; 
Upstart jumped 7% June 16 after a Needham reaffirmed Buy and $37 PT, closing at $32.36
, but 
a securities lawsuit notice was issued June 17
, creating a mixed picture and overhang into Thursday.
Defensive oncology mid-cap with no fresh catalyst identified; risk-on regime today actually argues against defensive biotech rotation. Weak signal setup.

GLP-1/obesity mid-cap with high-beta range; 
trading at $29.33, down 17.2% YTD heading into Q3 VK2735 maintenance data readout
, and 
Lake Street initiated Buy with $89 PT on May 28
. No stale catalyst today, fits the risk-on bounce regime.
